mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents through standardized communication protocols. It simplifies deployment complexity, enhances security, and reduces operational overhead for agent-based applications. Ideal for teams looking to streamline AI agent management in production environments.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is necessary for interacting with the Kagenti platform.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary for Linux.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Change the region as needed.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to use the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have the correct permissions to access the cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Install Kagenti on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step installs Kagenti on your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti --create-namespace

```

> Add the Kagenti Helm repository.
> Install Kagenti in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply the autoscaling configuration.
> Apply the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti installation from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> This command removes the Kagenti release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. This step will upgrade Kagenti to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kagenti -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.status.phase == "Running") | .metadata.name' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti

kubectl get pods -n kagenti

```

> Drain all running pods before upgrade.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n kagenti -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.status.phase == "Running") | .metadata.name' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes cluster unreachable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig may not be set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1' to reset kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install chart: ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm chart version may not be compatible with your Kubernetes version.

**Fix:**
Ensure you are using a compatible Helm chart version with your Kubernetes version.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be an issue with the application configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU or memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new agent communication protocols and enhanced security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n kagenti' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.
